rig.title: Radio Rig Control Setup
rig.subtitle: Configure and manage radio transceiver connections for automated frequency control during satellite tracking. This system uses Hamlib, the industry-standard library supporting over 200 radio models including popular brands like Yaesu, Icom, Kenwood, Elecraft, FlexRadio, and many others. Radio control is supported exclusively through network connections - direct serial/USB connections are not supported. Rigctld handles client requests via TCP sockets, allowing multiple user programs to share one radio. Set up connection parameters including host address, port, radio type, and VFO configuration. Rigs integrate seamlessly with satellite tracking to automatically adjust frequencies based on Doppler shift calculations.
rig.name: 名称
rig.radio_mode_help_monitor: Receive-only tracking. Only downlink Doppler corrections are applied.
rig.radio_mode_help_uplink_only: Transmit-path only. Only uplink Doppler corrections are applied.
rig.radio_mode_help_simplex: Single active path. One selected VFO is tuned; the other is left unchanged.
rig.radio_mode_help_duplex: Full satellite duplex. Both uplink and downlink paths are corrected.
rig.radio_mode_help_ptt_guarded: Like duplex, but during TX it freezes uplink retunes and keeps downlink updates.
rig.tx_mode_help_auto: Auto-selects the best strategy. Prefers split TX commands, then explicit VFO commands, and falls back to VFO switching.
rig.tx_mode_help_vfo_switch: Updates each path by switching VFO context and tuning frequencies per VFO.
rig.tx_mode_help_split_tx_cmd: Uses direct RX/TX frequency commands (F/I) without regular VFO switching when supported.
rig.tx_mode_help_vfo_explicit: Uses explicit VFO-targeted tuning (VFOA/VFOB) to reduce VFO-state ambiguity on some rigs.
rig.retune_interval_help: How often the rig frequency retune logic runs. Lower values track faster but increase rig command load.
rig.follow_downlink_tuning_help: Learns manual downlink tuning offset and applies matching correction to uplink/downlink while tracking.
rig.confirm_delete_message: Are you sure you want to delete the selected rig(s)?
rig.no_rigs: No radio rigs configured. Click 'Add' to configure a rig.

rotator.title: Antenna Rotator Control Setup
rotator.subtitle: Configure and manage antenna rotator systems for automated satellite tracking. This system uses Hamlib, supporting a wide range of rotator controllers including Yaesu G-5500, Alfa SPID, M2 RC2800, Hy-Gain DCU-1, and many others. Define connection parameters, azimuth and elevation limits, rotation types, and endstop configurations to ensure safe operation within your antenna system's mechanical constraints. Rotators integrate seamlessly with satellite tracking to automatically position antennas for optimal signal reception throughout satellite passes, with real-time status monitoring and safety limit enforcement.
rotator.name: 名称
rotator.park_position_helper: Optional. Leave both empty to use the rotator's native park behavior.
rotator.park_override_warning: Warning: enabling fixed park coordinates can move hardware unexpectedly. Use only if you trust the configured park angles.
rotator.azimuth_mode_help_0_360: Sends standard positive azimuth values in the 0 to 360 range (default behavior).
rotator.azimuth_mode_help_neg180_180: Sends values above 180 as negative (e.g. 270 -> -90) to reduce north-crossing unwind on supported rotators/controllers.
rotator.tolerance_warning: Tolerance settings control the deadband for rotator movement. Setting these values too low can cause excessive commands, jitter, or errors on some hardware. Default safe value is 2°.
rotator.tolerance_helper: Lower values may stress some rotators.
rotator.confirm_delete_message: Are you sure you want to delete the selected item(s)?

validation.min_az_lte_max_az: Min azimuth must be <= max azimuth
validation.min_el_lte_max_el: Min elevation must be <= max elevation
validation.park_both_or_none: Park azimuth and elevation must both be set or both left empty

rotator.no_rotators: No antenna rotators configured. Click 'Add' to configure a rotator.

camera.title: Camera Integration Setup
camera.subtitle: Configure camera streams for visual monitoring during satellite tracking operations. Currently, only WebRTC is fully supported for real-time, low-latency streaming. For RTSP camera sources, consider using go2rtc, which is now built into Home Assistant 2024.11 and later. Go2rtc provides a comprehensive streaming solution supporting RTSP, WebRTC, HomeKit, FFmpeg, and RTMP formats. Add, edit, and manage camera connections with custom names and stream URLs for integration with your ground station setup.
camera.name: 名称
camera.confirm_delete_message: Are you sure you want to delete the selected item(s)?

sdr.title: Software Defined Radio (SDR) Configuration
sdr.subtitle: Configure Software Defined Radio devices for receiving satellite signals. This system supports RTL-SDR dongles (both v3 and v4 models), SoapySDR devices (both local USB and remote network devices), and USRP devices via UHD. SDRs can be connected locally via USB or remotely over TCP/IP for flexible ground station configurations. Define device parameters including supported frequency ranges, network addresses for remote devices, driver specifications for SoapySDR, and serial numbers for device identification.
sdr.name: 名称
sdr.confirm_delete_message: Are you sure you want to delete the selected item(s)?
sdr.servers_subtitle: The following SoapySDR server instances were discovered on the local network using mDNS/Zeroconf service discovery. These servers are advertising their availability for remote SDR connections.
sdr.no_soapy_devices: No local SoapySDR devices detected. Please connect a device and refresh.
sdr.no_rtl_devices: No local RTL-SDR devices detected. Please connect a device and refresh.
sdr.no_sdrs: No SDR devices configured. Click 'Add' to configure an SDR.
